2604.23724.title: Zoom In, Reason Out: Efficient Far-field Anomaly Detection in Expressway Surveillance Videos via Focused VLM Reasoning Guided by Bayesian Inference
2604.23724.paper_summary: Expressway video anomaly detection is essential for safety management. However, identifying anomalies across diverse scenes remains challenging, particularly for far-field targets exhibiting subtle abnormal vehicle motions. While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) demonstrate strong semantic reasoning capabilities, processing global frames causes attention dilution for these far-field objects and incurs prohibitive computational costs. To address these issues, we propose VIBES, an asynchronous collaborative framework utilizing VLMs guided by Bayesian inference. Specifically, to overcome poor generalization across varying expressway environments, we introduce an online Bayesian inference module. This module continuously evaluates vehicle trajectories to dynamically update the probabilistic boundaries of normal driving behaviors, serving as an asynchronous trigger to precisely localize anomalies in space and time. Instead of processing the continuous video stream, the VLM processes only the localized visual regions indicated by the trigger. This targeted visual input prevents attention dilution and enables accurate semantic reasoning. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that VIBES improves detection accuracy for far-field anomalies and reduces computational overhead, achieving high real-time efficiency and explainability while demonstrating generalization across diverse expressway conditions.
2604.23724.paper_summary_zh: 高速公路視頻異常檢測對於安全管理至關重要。<lb/>然而，在多樣化場景中識別異常仍然具有挑戰性，特別是對於表現出微妙異常車輛運動的遠場目標。<lb/>雖然視覺-語言模型（VLMs）展現出強大的語義推理能力，但處理全局幀會導致這些遠場物體的注意力稀釋，並產生高昂的計算成本。<lb/>為了解決這些問題，我們提出了VIBES，一個利用貝葉斯推理指導的非同步協作框架，並使用VLMs。<lb/>具體來說，為了克服在不同高速公路環境中較差的泛化能力，我們引入了一個在線貝葉斯推理模塊。<lb/>該模塊不斷評估車輛軌跡，以動態更新正常駕駛行為的概率邊界，作為一個非同步觸發器，精確定位空間和時間中的異常。<lb/>VLM僅處理觸發器所指示的本地化視覺區域，而不是處理連續視頻流。<lb/>這種針對性的視覺輸入防止了注意力的稀釋，並使準確的語義推理成為可能。<lb/>廣泛的評估顯示，VIBES提高了遠場異常的檢測準確性並減少了計算開銷，實現了高實時效率和可解釋性，同時在多樣化的高速公路條件下展現了良好的泛化能力。
2604.23724.author: Xiaowei Mao et.al.
2604.23724.authors: Xiaowei Mao, Bowen Sui, Weijie Zhang, Yawen Yang, Shengnan Guo, Shilong Zhao, Jiaqi Lin, Tingrui Wu, Youfang Lin, Huaiyu Wa
